channel.title: Forge
channel.description: Updates for Forge — agent-first development workbench from Coursion.

item.title: Version 0.6.9
item.description: Install bundled agent skills directly from the ⌘K command palette (Skills section), in addition to Settings.

item.title: Version 0.6.8
item.description:
2026-06-09

  • Install agent skills — Forge now ships its Git workflow skills (commit, push to a feature branch, merge worktrees, merge to main) and can install them into your agent's global skills directory. Settings → Agents → Agent skills, one click per agent (Claude Code, Hermes) — every project gets them, no manual setup.

item.title: Version 0.6.7
item.description:
2026-06-08

  • Readable update notes — The "A new version is available" dialog now renders release notes as formatted text — bold feature names, bullet lists, and links — instead of raw Markdown symbols.

item.title: Version 0.6.6
item.description:
2026-06-07

  • Categorize workspaces — Group workspaces into collapsible sidebar categories. Drag a workspace onto a category header to file it, or use the new Categorize menu on any workspace. Rename or dissolve categories from the header's context menu.
  • Auto-categorize — One click on the wand button sorts ungrouped workspaces by project type (iOS, macOS, Web, Mobile, Backend, Scripts) — detected locally from project files, no AI key needed.

item.title: Version 0.6.5
item.description:
2026-06-07

  • No more false "finished" — Builds and scripts that go quiet mid-run no longer ping as finished. Silence-based turn detection now applies only to TUI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Aider…); ordinary commands signal completion when they actually exit.
  • Apple Intelligence board planning works — AI Fill on the planning board no longer fails with the on-device model; project context is now trimmed to fit its context window, and failure alerts show the real provider error.
  • Cleaner sidebar — Removed the per-workspace activity pill from the sidebar list (still shown in the top tab strip).
  • No more orphaned shells — Closing a terminal pane, deleting a workspace, or quitting Forge now terminates the underlying shell process instead of leaving it running in the background.

item.title: Version 0.5.1
item.description: Fixed: Claude Code and Hermes agent chips no longer vanish from the toolbar. Agent detection now scans PATH directly with retries instead of relying on a one-shot login-shell check.

item.title: Version 0.5.0
item.description:
2026-06-05

  • Prompt Optimizer — Rewrite any prompt with project-aware context before sending it to an agent. Get a side-by-side comparison plus a model and effort recommendation. Trigger from the chat input bar, the command palette, or ⌘⇧O.
  • Frecency-ranked ⌘K — The command palette now ranks board cards, workspace panes, and recent files by how often and how recently you use them.
  • Searchable Add popover — Adding a pane is a quick type-to-filter popover instead of a nested menu, with full pane names visible.
  • Reliable agent chips — Claude and Hermes toolbar chips no longer vanish after launch; agent detection now retries transient failures and caches results.
  • Responsive toolbar — The workspace top bar adapts to narrow windows with a compact mode instead of clipping buttons.
  • Updater fixes — In-app updates now wire the Sparkle delegate correctly and skip update checks when running off the launch path.

item.title: Version 0.4.1
item.description:
2026-06-04

  • Worktree badge — Every terminal header and the workspace toolbar now show the active git branch and worktree at a glance, targeting the focused terminal.
  • Per-terminal worktree toggle — Enable or disable isolated worktree mode for the focused terminal straight from the toolbar.
  • Reliable git detection — Reworked git invocation to exec directly with a composed PATH and retry transient spawn failures, fixing flaky branch/worktree detection under load.
  • Faster dev loop — Reliable rebuild-and-relaunch flow with InjectionIII hot reload.

item.title: Version 0.3.0
item.description:
2026-06-02

  • In-app updates — Forge now checks for, downloads, and installs new versions itself via Sparkle. Auto-check and auto-download are toggleable in Settings, with last-checked status and a one-click Check for Updates.
  • What's New — A release-notes modal driven by the bundled changelog greets you after each update, with an icon-row layout that turns each highlight into a glanceable card.
  • Selectable terminal output — Select and copy text from a terminal while a command is still streaming.
  • Pane & terminal rename — Give any pane or terminal a custom name straight from its tab.
  • Workspace icon picker — Choose from 103 SF Symbols when customizing a workspace.
  • Settings refresh — Branded toggles and segmented pickers throughout, a dedicated Coach tab, and collapsible cards to cut first-glance clutter.

item.title: Version 0.2.0
item.description: Planning board: per-workspace Trello-style kanban with AI fill, live two-way file sync, and structured sync endpoints (.forge/board.json + board.inbox.jsonl) so agents read/mutate the board without parsing markdown. Send cards straight to agent terminals. Telegram gateway + Hermes push notifications. Vendored SwiftTerm fork with Warp-style inline path completion and reliable command-completion detection. Unified solid-surface button styling.
